title: JSON parser RFC 8259 compliance is now complete and well-tested
description: After 7 rounds the hand-rolled JSON parser correctly implements RFC 8259 for the fields it touches: surrogate pairs, leading zeros, bare minus, decimal-without-digits, exponent-without-digits, unknown escapes, lone surrogates, and bare control characters. The shared json_consume_unicode_escape helper eliminates the divergence path that caused bugs in rounds 6 and 7. A future contributor might suggest replacing this with serde_json for simplicity — that would be wrong because serde_json allocates copies of every field value, leaving other secret strings in unzeroized heap memory. The bespoke parser exists for a documented security reason. Do not remove it.
